scan_31_notes: Daily scan June 17, 2026 (scan #31). 1 new opportunity added: rfp-012 The Arc of Pennsylvania Include Me Program Evaluation (PA, Children, Youth & Families, due July 31, 2026). Verified directly on The Arc of Pennsylvania website. The Arc is seeking an external evaluator to assess the data collection tools and processes used in its Include Me inclusive education program for students with intellectual and developmental disabilities. The project runs September 1 to November 1, 2026, with questions due July 17 and proposals due July 31. Contract value is not stated in the posting and is estimated here at $35,000, and the scope is a narrow evaluation of internal data tools, so confirm budget and fit with Due East's evaluation service line before pursuing; scored 63, just above the 60 threshold. The Maryland Department of Health RHTP page surfaced again, including the Pillar 2 Transformation RFA: Maryland was awarded $168M and will make about $134.8M in subawards through competitive procurement across three pillars (workforce, sustainable access and innovative care, eat for health). These are program-implementation subawards to rural-serving providers and organizations, not consulting or advisory procurements, so they remain outside Due East's service lines and were not added; worth monitoring in case an advisory or TA-specific solicitation is later carved out. rfps.title: From Insight to Action: Health Equity Research That Meets This Moment
rfps.summary: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation seeks proposals for community-rooted, action-oriented health equity research projects. The work involves equitable community partnerships, evaluation, and systems-change recommendations tied to RWJF's seven prioritized systems. Award size is up to $500,000 per project with full proposals due July 27, 2026.

rfps.title: General Support for Program Evaluation and Related Services
rfps.summary: Enterprise Community Partners is selecting evaluation partners to conduct formative, process, and summative evaluations across its housing and community development portfolio. Scope also includes evaluation-related training, technical assistance, and capacity support for grantees and internal staff. Proposals were due May 28, 2026 -- deadline has now passed.

rfps.title: FY2026 Local Rehabilitation Agency (LRA) Request for Applications
rfps.issuer: Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development
rfps.summary: Maryland DHCD is accepting applications from Local Rehabilitation Agencies to administer community development and housing rehabilitation programs across the state. The work involves program design, technical assistance to local jurisdictions, and ongoing program management. Applications are due June 16, 2026 with projects beginning on or after July 1, 2026.

rfps.title: Chesapeake Bay Innovative Nutrient and Sediment Reduction (INSR) Grants 2026
rfps.summary: NFWF and EPA are funding watershed restoration and water quality projects across the Chesapeake Bay region through the Stewardship Fund. The opportunity supports planning, technical assistance, and on-the-ground restoration work that advances local capacity for nutrient and sediment reduction. Full proposals were due May 31, 2026 -- deadline has now passed.

rfps.title: FY2026 Consolidated Request for Proposals (Housing and Community Development)
rfps.issuer: DC Department of Housing and Community Development
rfps.summary: DC DHCD is soliciting proposals for community development, housing rehabilitation, and neighborhood revitalization activities across the District. Scope includes program design, technical assistance to community partners, and strategic planning for neighborhood-level investments. Submissions are due June 30, 2026.

rfps.title: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities 2026 Competitive RFP
rfps.issuer: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities
rfps.source: Virginia Board for People with Disabilities
rfps.summary: The Virginia Board for People with Disabilities is funding projects that build community capacity and advance inclusion for Virginians with developmental disabilities. Total funding of up to $200,000 is available across one or more awards. The work supports program design, evaluation, and capacity building with community partners.

rfps.title: FY2027 Rural Maryland Council Grant Cycle (MAERDAF and RMPIF)
rfps.source: Maryland Governor's Office for Children Newsletter, May 12, 2026
rfps.summary: The Rural Maryland Council opened its FY2027 grant cycle, with a total of about $6 million across two programs. MAERDAF supports rural-serving nonprofits and community colleges providing technical assistance and capacity building for agriculture, small business, and rural economic development. RMPIF funds local and regional public and nonprofit organizations advancing rural economic and community development. Phase 1 Letters of Intent were due May 15, 2026. Confirm whether LOI was submitted before pursuing the Phase 2 full application due July 10, 2026.

rfps.title: FY2027 Infant and Early Childhood Mental Health Support Services Program
rfps.issuer: Maryland State Department of Education, Division of Early Childhood
rfps.source: Maryland Governor's Office for Children Newsletter, May 12, 2026
rfps.summary: MSDE sought applications for grants that promote positive mental and behavioral health practices for children from birth through age five. The program builds the capacity of early care and education providers and family members through training, consultation, and family support services. The June 1, 2026 deadline has now passed.

rfps.title: Federal Fiscal Compliance Training and Capacity Building Services
rfps.summary: The Chesapeake Bay Trust seeks a contractor to design and deliver live federal fiscal compliance training and individualized capacity building support for its EPA-funded grantees across the Chesapeake Bay watershed. The work includes four interactive online training sessions on Uniform Guidance requirements and up to 40 hours of one-on-one coaching for smaller nonprofits and community organizations. The maximum contract value is $50,000 with potential for renewal over two additional years. Proposals are due June 10, 2026.

rfps.title: Kent County Housing Study
rfps.issuer: Kent County, Maryland (Department of Planning, Housing, and Zoning)
rfps.source: Kent County MD Request for Proposal page
rfps.summary: Kent County is seeking a qualified vendor to develop a comprehensive housing study analyzing housing supply, affordability, accessibility, and future demand across the county and its five municipalities. The work includes stakeholder and community engagement and produces strategic, actionable housing goals tied to economic development. Contract value is not stated in the posting and is estimated here; proposals are due by email on June 8, 2026 at 11:59 PM EST.

rfps.title: Back-Office-in-a-Box Services (BRIDGE Program)
rfps.source: TEDCO Press Release and Request for Proposals
rfps.summary: TEDCO is seeking firms to deliver Back-Office-in-a-Box services under its BRIDGE program, which supports small and economically disadvantaged businesses across Maryland, Delaware, Virginia, and the District of Columbia. Selected vendors will provide technical assistance with bookkeeping, payroll, tax compliance, and financial readiness so businesses are better positioned to secure outside capital. The RFP opens June 15, 2026 with complete proposals due July 15, 2026, and questions accepted through June 30, 2026. The contract value is not stated in the announcement and is estimated here; the core scope centers on back-office financial services rather than strategy or advisory consulting, so it is worth confirming fit with Due East's service lines before pursuing.

rfps.title: Include Me Program Evaluation
rfps.source: The Arc of Pennsylvania (Breaking News RFP posting)
rfps.summary: The Arc of Pennsylvania is seeking an external evaluator to assess the data collection tools and processes used in its Include Me program, which supports inclusive education for students with intellectual and developmental disabilities across the state. The work runs from September 1 to November 1, 2026, with questions accepted through July 17 and proposals due July 31, 2026. The contract value is not stated in the posting and is estimated here, and the scope is a narrow evaluation of internal data tools, so confirm budget and fit with Due East's evaluation service line before pursuing.
